sarga.title: Lakshmana asks Rama to bear sorrow -- pleads with Rama to think rightly-- arouses Rama's wisdom.

shlokas.original:
तं तथा शोकसन्तप्तं विलपन्तमनाथवत्।
मोहेन महताविष्टं परिद्यूनमचेतनम्।।3.66.1।।
ततस्सौमित्रिराश्वास्य मुहूर्तादिव लक्ष्मणः।
रामं सम्बोधयामास चरणौ चाभिपीडयन्।।3.66.2।।
shlokas.translation: Lakshmana, son of Sumitra, consoling the mighty miserable Rama, who was afflicted with sorrow, and was weeping helplessly like an orphan, drowned in great delusion, devoid of consciousness, pressed his feet and addressed him:

shlokas.original:
महता तपसा राम महता चापि कर्मणा।
राज्ञा दशरथेनासि लब���धोऽमृतमिवामरैः।।3.66.3।।
shlokas.translation: O Rama king Dasaratha begot you by doing a great penance and performing great deeds, just as gods begot the nectar.

shlokas.original:
तव चैव गुणैर्बद्धस्त्वद्वियोगान्महीपतिः।
राजा देवत्वमापन्नो भरतस्य यथाश्रुतम्।।3.66.4।।
shlokas.translation: King Dasaratha, impelled by your virtues, and separation from you attained godhood as heard from Bharata.

shlokas.original:
यदि दुःखमिदं प्राप्तं काकुत्स्थ न सहिष्यसे।
प्राकृतश्चाल्पसत्त्वश्च इतरः कस्सहिष्यति।।3.66.5।।
shlokas.translation: O Rama, if you cannot bear this sorrow that has befallen you, how can weak and ordinary persons endure (such suffering)?

shlokas.original:
दुःखितो हि भवान्लोकांस्तेजसा यदि धक्ष्यते।
आर्ताः प्रजा नरव्याघ्र क्व नु यास्यन्ति निर्वृतिम्।।3.66.6।।
shlokas.translation: O best of men  if you grieve and burn the world with your brilliance, wherefrom will the (ordinary) afflicted people get relief?

shlokas.original:
लोकस्वभाव एवैष ययातिर्नहुषात्मजः।
गतश्शक्रेण सालोक्यमनयस्तं समस्पृशत्।।3.66.7।।
shlokas.translation: It is the go of the world. Yayati, son of Nahusa, shared heaven with Indra but because of his curse (for his bad conduct) suffered misfortune.

shlokas.original:
महर्षिर्यो वसिष्ठस्तु यः पितुर्नः पुरोहितः।
अह्ना पुत्रशतं जज्ञे तथैवास्य पुनर्हतम्।।3.66.8।।
shlokas.translation: To sage Vasistha, our father's priest, were born a hundred sons in a day.But all of them were killed in one day the same way.

shlokas.original:
या चेयं जगतां माता देवी लोकनमस्कृता।
अस्याश्च चलनं भूमेर्दृश्यते सत्यसंश्रव।।3.66.9।।
shlokas.translation: You (rigidly) adhere to truth.(Like you) the mother earth who is adored by all the worlds, is unshakeable. Yet she is seen to be shaking at times.

shlokas.original:
यौ धर्मौ जगतां नेत्रौ यत्र सर्वं प्रतिष्ठितम्।
आदित्यचन्द्रौ ग्रहणमभ्युपेतौ महाबलौ।।3.66.10।।
shlokas.translation: The powerful Sun and Moon, righteous entities, who are the two eyes of these worlds and on whom everything rests firmly, also suffer eclipses sometimes.

shlokas.original:
सुमहान्त्यपि भूतानि देवाश्च पुरुषर्षभ।
न दैवस्य प्रमुञ्चन्ति सर्वभूतानि देहिनः।।3.66.11।।
shlokas.translation: O best among men  Fate spares none. Great beings, every creature, even the gods cannot escape fate.

shlokas.original:
शक्रादिष्वपि देवेषु वर्तमानौ नयानयौ।
श्रूयेते नरशार्दूल न त्वं शोचितुमर्हसि।।3.66.12।।
shlokas.translation: It is I heard that Indra and other deities are also governed by the principle of justice. Therefore, you should not bewail, O tiger among men.

shlokas.original:
नष्टायामपि वैदेह्यां हृतायामपि चानघ
शोचितुं नार्हसे वीर यथान्यः प्राकृतस्तथा।।3.66.13।।
shlokas.translation: Even if Vaidehi is killed or abducted, you should not grieve like ordinary men, O  sinless hero

shlokas.original:
त्वव्दिधा न हि शोचन्ति सततं सत्यदर्शिनः।
सुमहत्स्वपि कृच्छ्रेषु रामानिर्विण्णदर्शनाः।।3.66.14।।
shlokas.translation: O Rama, you always see the truth. So  in the face of great difficulties men like you, whose vision is not blurred, should not lose heart.

shlokas.original:
तत्त्वतो हि नरश्रेष्ठ बुद्ध्या समनुचिन्तय।
बुध्द्या युक्ता महाप्राज्ञा विजानन्ति शुभाशुभे।।3.66.15।।
shlokas.translation: O best among men  judge the facts rightly with your intellect. The wise apply their minds and know what is right and what is wrong.

shlokas.original:
अदृष्टगुणदोषाणामध्रुवाणां तु कर्मणाम्।
नान्तरेण क्रियां तेषां फलमिष्टं प्रवर्तते।।3.66.16।।
shlokas.translation: Good results are not expected from the actions of persons who cannot distinguish between their virtues and vices, from those who are unstable in their deeds.

shlokas.original:
त्वमेव हि पुरा राम मामेवं बहुशोऽन्वशाः।
अनुशिष्याद्धि को नु त्वामपि साक्षाद्बृहस्पतिः।।3.66.17।।
shlokas.translation: O Rama you yourself in the past have instructed me many times that way. Who can be your adviser in this matter ? You are indeed like Brihaspati.

shlokas.original:
बुद्धिश्च ते महाप्राज्ञ देवैरपि दुरन्वया।
शोकेनाभिप्रसुप्तं ते ज्ञानं सम्बोधयाम्यहम्।।3.66.18।।
shlokas.translation: Even gods cannot fathom your intellect. I am only trying to rouse your wisdom your sorrow has sent to sleep.

shlokas.original:
दिव्यं च मानुषं च त्वमात्मनश्च पराक्रमम्।
इक्ष्वाकुवृषभावेक्ष्य यतस्व द्विषतां वधे।।3.66.19।।
shlokas.translation: O bull among the Ikshvakus, weigh your divine and human prowess before you try to kill your enemies.

shlokas.original:
किं तेन सर्वनाशेन कृतेन पुरुषर्षभ।
तमेव त्वं रिपुं पापं विज्ञायोद्धर्तुमर्हसि।।3.66.20।।
shlokas.translation: O best among men  where is the good in the destruction of all? Know your sinful enemy before you uproot him. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकेय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे षट्षष्टितमस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the sixtysixth sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
